questions.text: What is the primary effect of a poet using an end-stopped line?
options: To speed up the reading pace.
To force a moment of reflection and slow the rhythm.
To make the poem rhyme better.
questions.explanation: End-stopped lines create natural pauses, forcing the reader to stop and consider the preceding thought.

questions.text: Which of these is TRUE analysis rather than 'feature spotting'?
options: The poet uses a simile when they say 'like a ghost'.
The simile 'like a ghost' shows that the character is pale.
The simile 'like a ghost' connotes erasure and marginalization, suggesting the character has been stripped of their identity and societal presence.
questions.explanation: Option 3 identifies the technique (simile), analyzes the connotations (erasure), and connects it to a deeper meaning (societal presence).
